title: Flaky Peppery Algerian Coconut Milk Tagine
description: A savory Algerian dish with the perfect balance of spice and flaky texture, featuring unsweetened coconut milk. This recipe is perfect for a sick day, and can be cooked in under an hour.
instructions: In a large bowl, mix together the chicken, cumin, paprika, black pepper, and salt. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes.
Heat the oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the chicken and cook until browned on both sides, about 5-7 minutes.
Transfer the chicken to a large pot or Dutch oven. Add the coconut milk, chicken broth, flour, and pumpkin seeds. Stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 20-25 minutes.
While the stew is cooking, prepare the Agave leaves by cutting them into large pieces and removing the stems.
After the stew has cooked for 20-25 minutes, carefully place 2-3 Agave leaves in the pot, depending on their size.
Cover the pot with a lid and continue to simmer for another 5-10 minutes. The Agave leaves should be tender and flaky.
To serve, place a piece of Agave-leaf-wrapped chicken on each plate and garnish with parsley and cilantro.
Serve hot, with additional black pepper to taste.
Garnish with additional pumpkin seeds, if desired.
